timeLock.shortExplanationDetail: Even if there's unauthorized access, 24 hours gives you time to detect and respond.
timeLock.modalIntro: Time Lock is a critical security feature that protects your assets. This 24-hour 'thinking time' allows you to detect and respond to unauthorized access.
timeLock.protection1: Unauthorized access detection: Even if someone steals your private key and tries to unlock, 24 hours gives you time to notice and respond
timeLock.protection2: Phishing protection: Even if you accidentally sign something, you have 24 hours before assets actually move
timeLock.protection3: Hacking protection: Attackers cannot withdraw assets within 24 hours
timeLock.waitingTitle: What you can do during the wait
timeLock.waiting3: Use Emergency Unlock if needed (7-day wait + Bond required)
timeLock.tipsContent1: This attack involves collecting encrypted data now and decrypting it with future quantum computers. Current ECDSA signatures may be breakable by quantum computers in 10-15 years. This means today's transaction data could become a future threat.
timeLock.tipsContent2: Quantum-resistant cryptography (Dilithium) has mathematical foundations that cannot be broken by future quantum computers. Combined with 24-hour Time Lock, even if keys are leaked, you have time to detect and respond. This dual defense protects your assets now and in the future.

meta.title: Quantum Shield - Protect Your Assets with Quantum-Resistant Cryptography
meta.description: The world's first quantum-resistant cryptographic bridge combining Dilithium-III and SPHINCS+. Protect your assets today from tomorrow's threats.

hero.subtitle: Protect your ETH and tokens from future quantum computer attacks. Secure your assets with NIST-certified next-generation cryptography. Get started in 3 simple steps.

dilithium.title: Dilithium-III Cryptography
dilithium.description: NIST-certified lattice-based digital signatures. Next-generation cryptography that is mathematically resistant to quantum computers.
dilithium.tooltip: Dilithium is a digital signature algorithm that is secure against quantum computers. It was standardized by NIST in 2024.

timeLock.title: Time Lock Protection
timeLock.description: 24-hour security waiting period. A temporal buffer to detect and prevent unauthorized access.
timeLock.tooltip: Time Lock provides a 24-hour waiting period before asset withdrawal. This allows time to detect and prevent fraud.

smtProof.title: SMT Merkle Verification
smtProof.description: Cryptographic proofs via Sparse Merkle Trees. All state transitions are verifiable on-chain, combined with SPHINCS+ multi-sig for layered defense.
smtProof.tooltip: SMT (Sparse Merkle Tree) efficiently verifies large-scale data integrity. Combined with SHA3-256 hashing to achieve quantum resistance.

selfCustody.title: Self-Custody
selfCustody.description: You alone control your assets. Private keys are generated and stored locally, never sent to servers.
selfCustody.tooltip: Self-custody means managing your own assets without relying on third parties.

emergency.title: Emergency Recovery
emergency.description: Peace of mind even if you lose your private key. A safety net to recover assets with a 7-day wait and bond.
emergency.tooltip: Emergency recovery allows you to recover assets by depositing collateral, even if you lose your private key.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: All transactions are verifiable on-chain. Open-source, audited smart contracts.
transparency.tooltip: Transparency means all transaction history and smart contract code is publicly available and verifiable by anyone. Third-party audits have also been completed.

meta.title: FAQ | Quantum Shield
meta.description: Frequently asked questions about Quantum Shield

whatIsQs.question: What is Quantum Shield?
whatIsQs.answer: Quantum Shield is a 'vault' that cannot be opened even by quantum computers. When you put your crypto assets (like ETH) in this vault, no one can take them out without a special key (Dilithium key). This key is designed to be unbreakable by future quantum computers. Plus, the vault guardians (Provers) are forced to be honest through penalty mechanisms.

whyQuantum.question: Why is quantum resistance important?
whyQuantum.answer: Current crypto signatures (ECDSA) could be broken by quantum computers in 10-15 years. The problem is 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks—malicious actors collect encrypted data now and decrypt it all at once with future quantum computers. Your transaction data could be targeted. Using quantum-resistant cryptography now prevents future data leakage risks.

whatIsLock.question: What is Lock?
whatIsLock.answer: Lock means putting your assets into a 'vault' that quantum computers cannot open. This vault can only be opened with your personal Dilithium key. Without your key, no one (not even Quantum Shield operators) can move your assets. This is the essence of self-custody.

why24h.question: Why does Unlock take 24 hours?
why24h.answer: The 24-hour Time Lock is 'thinking time.' Even if someone steals your key and tries to unlock, 24 hours gives you time to notice and respond. You can issue a Challenge during this period to stop fraudulent unlocks. Assets not moving instantly actually increases security.

whatIsEmergency.question: What is Emergency Unlock?
whatIsEmergency.answer: A safety net when you lose your Dilithium private key. Deposit a Bond (collateral) and wait 7 days, then you can recover assets with just a wallet signature. The Bond is fully refunded after 7 days for legitimate unlocks. For fraudulent attempts, the Bond is confiscated and paid to the Challenger. This eliminates incentives for fraud.

lostKey.question: What should I do if I lose my secret key?
lostKey.answer: Use Emergency Unlock. Pay a Bond (MAX(0.5 ETH, amount×5%)) and wait 7 days to recover assets. The 7 days is a 'challenge period' where anyone can raise a 'this is fraud' Challenge. If no issues, Bond is fully refunded. If fraudulent, it's confiscated.

whatIsProver.question: What is a Prover?
whatIsProver.answer: A Prover is a 'vault guardian.' When you request an unlock, the Prover generates a ZK-STARK proof to mathematically verify the request is legitimate. Multiple Provers exist and are randomly selected via VRF (Verifiable Random Function), making it difficult for any specific Prover to commit fraud. Provers who commit fraud have their stake (collateral) confiscated.

howItWorks.question: How does it work technically?
howItWorks.answer: ①User signs unlock request with Dilithium → ②VRF randomly selects Prover → ③Prover generates ZK-STARK proof → ④Prover signs with SPHINCS+ → ⑤24h Time Lock → ⑥L3 verification, SMT proof recorded on L1 → ⑦Assets return to wallet. This multi-layer structure has no single attack point and is resistant to future quantum computers.

hero.title: Quantum-Resistant Cryptography
hero.subtitle: Protecting assets from future quantum computer attacks

dilithium.title: Dilithium-III
dilithium.description: NIST-certified lattice-based digital signature algorithm. Used for user asset lock/unlock. Mathematically difficult to break even with quantum computers.

sphincs.title: SPHINCS+
sphincs.description: Hash-based digital signatures. Used for Prover (approver) signatures. Enables multi-layer defense with different mathematical foundations.

smtProof.title: SMT Merkle Proof
smtProof.description: Cryptographic proofs via Sparse Merkle Trees. State transitions (SR_0→SR_1) are calculated with SHA3-256 hashing, all verifiable on-chain.

lockProcessing.errorRevert: Transaction reverted on L1. This may be due to insufficient amount (minimum 0.01 ETH) or TVL cap exceeded.

errors.walletNotConnected: Wallet not connected. Please connect your wallet and try again.
errors.dilithiumKeyFailed: Failed to get Dilithium public key

steps.generateKeysTooltip: Generate a Dilithium-III (ML-DSA-65) quantum-resistant key pair. These keys are securely stored in your browser.
steps.signTooltip: Dilithium is a NIST-approved next-generation digital signature algorithm resistant to quantum computers. Your private key signs the lock request.
steps.submitL3Tooltip: L3 Aegis validates your signature and computes the State Root (SR_0). This eliminates the need to send your full Dilithium public key to L1, significantly reducing gas costs.
steps.submitL1Tooltip: Sign the transaction with your wallet (e.g., MetaMask) and submit to the Sepolia network. Only the lock_id and SR_0 from L3 are sent, keeping gas costs low.
steps.confirmTooltip: Waiting for the transaction to be confirmed on the Sepolia blockchain. This usually takes 1-2 minutes.

lockSuccess.title: Lock Complete
lockSuccess.subtitle: Your assets are now protected with quantum-resistant security

unlockProcessing.title: Processing Unlock...
unlockProcessing.subtitle: Please wait. Do not close this page.
unlockProcessing.emergencyWarning: Emergency unlock requires a 7-day waiting period and bond (minimum 0.5 ETH)
unlockProcessing.noKeysWarning: No Dilithium keys available. Please complete a lock operation first.

steps.loadLockTooltip: Fetch lock details from L3. Verify amount, owner, and status.
steps.signTooltip: Dilithium is a NIST-approved next-generation digital signature algorithm resistant to quantum computers. Your private key signs the unlock request.
steps.submitL3Tooltip: Send signed unlock request to L3 Aegis. L3 computes SR_1 and selects Provers via VRF.
steps.submitEmergencyTooltip: Send emergency unlock request with bond to L3 Aegis. A 7-day waiting period begins.
steps.vrfSelectionTooltip: Provers are randomly selected via Chainlink VRF. Selected provers provide SPHINCS+ signatures.
steps.bondVerifyTooltip: Confirm the emergency unlock bond (minimum 0.5 ETH) has been received correctly.
steps.activateTimelockTooltip: The 24-hour (normal) or 7-day (emergency) timelock is activated. Challenges can be submitted during this period.

unlockSuccess.title: Unlock Started
unlockSuccess.subtitle: After the 24-hour Time Lock, assets will be transferred to your wallet
unlockSuccess.subtitleEmergency: After the 7-day Time Lock, assets will be transferred to your wallet

emergency.warning: Emergency unlock requires a 7-day waiting period. If a Challenge is raised during this period, your bond may be forfeited.

info.message: If there are no issues during the security waiting period, assets will be automatically transferred to your wallet. You can track progress from the dashboard.

claim.ready: Time Lock has expired. You can now claim your assets.

meta.title: Terms of Service | Quantum Shield
meta.description: Quantum Shield Terms of Service

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your use of Quantum Shield ("Service"). By using the Service, you agree to be bound by these Terms.

service.title: 2. Service Description
service.content: The Service provides digital asset protection using quantum-resistant cryptography. You use the Service at your own risk and responsibility.

eligibility.title: 3. Eligibility
eligibility.content: The Service is available only to individuals 18 years or older, or legal entities. Access from certain countries or regions may be restricted.

risks.title: 4. Risks
risks.content: Cryptocurrency prices are volatile and you may lose your principal. The Service does not provide investment advice. You are responsible for your own decisions.

keys.title: 5. Private Key Management
keys.content: You are responsible for securely managing your Dilithium private key. We are not liable for any losses resulting from lost or compromised private keys.

prohibited.title: 6. Prohibited Activities
prohibited.content: Using the Service for unauthorized access, money laundering, terrorist financing, or any other illegal activities is prohibited.

disclaimer.title: 7. Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: The Service is provided "as is" without any warranties, express or implied. We are not liable for any damages resulting from smart contract vulnerabilities, network failures, or other technical issues.

changes.title: 8. Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may modify these Terms as necessary. Significant changes will be notified through the Service.

governing.title: 9. Governing Law
governing.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of Japan. The Tokyo District Court shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For inquiries regarding these Terms, please contact support@quantumshield.io

intro.title: About Cookies
intro.content: Quantum Shield ("we") uses cookies to improve user experience and enhance our services. This policy explains the types and purposes of cookies we use.

whatAreCookies.title: What Are Cookies
whatAreCookies.content: Cookies are small text files that websites store in your browser. They allow websites to remember your preferences and behavior, enabling better service delivery.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.content: These cookies are necessary for the basic functionality of the website. The service cannot work properly without them.

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.content: These cookies anonymously analyze service usage to help us improve.

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.content: These cookies provide additional features to enhance user experience.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.content: We use the following third-party services. Please refer to their respective privacy policies.

manage.title: Managing Cookies
manage.content: You can manage and delete cookies through your browser settings. However, disabling certain cookies may limit some service features.

changes.title: Policy Changes
changes.content: We may update this policy as necessary. Significant changes will be notified on the service.
